data.decision: Defer another website pilot; choose a go-workflow-stack exporter/importer or bridge-hardening slice as the next repo-local workflow adoption step.
rationale: viggomeesters.nl already proves repo-local .go execution, finish, fresh-clone validation, public repo-complete, and a real SEO task lifecycle.
The recent failure mode was routing/tooling confusion around go-task and AW Lite, not lack of another site pilot.
The next leverage point is stack-level portability: make repo-local state easier to author, export/import, inspect, and bridge without hand-written JSON or stale vault-first skill behavior.

suggested_acceptance: A fresh-clone repo-local project can export a compact task/vision/readback bundle without vault access.
The stack can import or reconcile that bundle into another clone or review artifact without corrupting existing .go state.
Routing docs/skills state clearly that repo-local .go wins when .go/project.json exists; AW Lite is fallback/control-plane only.

data.site_follow_up: No additional website-specific workflow adoption task is needed now; keep remaining site work as concrete public-site improvement tasks.
